The '''Stark Veteran Assembly''' ('''SVA''') was an organization composed of, and aimed to support and speak for, [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|Republic Outland Regions Security Force]] veterans of the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]]. Headquartered on the [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] city of [[Phelar/Legends|Phelar]], its spokesperson as of [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]] was [[Laslo Dorits]]. The SVA participated in the [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] [[Eriadu Trade Summit]], where they presumably spoke against letting the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] arm itself.

Backing [[Militarists/Legends|Militarists]], the SVA were devoted advocates for an establishment of a standing Republic military, especially in the lead-up to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and at least once publicly criticized [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] for her pacifist views, in particular her implication that those who advocate for war or serve in war are cowards. Dorits responded on the late night show ''[[Essence]]'' by stating that Amidala's statements were "disgraceful, stupid and typical", as well as pointing out that it was soldiers who had [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|liberated]] her home planet from Trade Federation [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|occupation]].

{{Company-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The [[2012]] guide book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] the Republic-affiliated paramilitary force that appeared in the [[2001]] comic arc ''[[Star Wars: The Stark Hyperspace War]]'' as being the [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|Outland Regions Security Force]].
